Strawberry & Balsamic Gazpacho.name: Strawberry & Balsamic Gazpacho
Strawberry & Balsamic Gazpacho.description: A refreshing, vibrant cold soup balancing the jammy sweetness of strawberries with the sharp acidity of aged balsamic.
instructions: Hull and roughly chop the strawberries.
Combine strawberries, cucumber, red bell pepper, and balsamic vinegar in a blender.
Blend on high until completely smooth.
With the blender running on low, slowly drizzle in the olive oil.
Chill for at least 2 hours before serving. Garnish with cracked black pepper.

Roasted Strawberry Meringue with Basil O.name: Roasted Strawberry Meringue with Basil Oil
Roasted Strawberry Meringue with Basil O.description: An elegant dessert featuring the concentrated caramel notes of roasted strawberries, contrasted with aromatic basil.
instructions: Toss strawberries in a tablespoon of sugar and roast at 350°F (175°C) for 20 minutes.
Make the basil oil by blanching basil leaves for 10 seconds, chilling in ice water, then blending with neutral oil and straining.
Whip egg whites to soft peaks, slowly add remaining sugar until stiff, glossy peaks form.
Bake meringue nests at 225°F (105°C) for 90 minutes until dry.
Serve meringues filled with roasted strawberries and drizzled with basil oil.

Strawberry-Black Pepper Shrub.name: Strawberry-Black Pepper Shrub
Strawberry-Black Pepper Shrub.description: A complex drinking vinegar that marries the sweet-tart profile of strawberries with the warming spice of black pepper.
ingredients_list: 1 tsp Whole Black Peppercorns, lightly crushed
instructions: Combine strawberries, sugar, and crushed black peppercorns in a bowl. Muddle slightly.
Cover and let sit at room temperature for 24-48 hours to create a syrup (oleo saccharum).
Strain the syrup, discarding the solids.
Stir the apple cider vinegar into the syrup.
Bottle and refrigerate for at least a week to mellow before using in cocktails or sodas.

Orange & Fennel Salad with Thyme Vinaigr.name: Orange & Fennel Salad with Thyme Vinaigrette
Orange & Fennel Salad with Thyme Vinaigr.description: A crisp, bright salad where the zesty citrus cuts through the licorice notes of the fennel.
instructions: Whisk olive oil, white wine vinegar, and fresh thyme to create the vinaigrette.
Layer shaved fennel on a serving platter.
Arrange orange segments over the fennel.
Drizzle generously with the thyme vinaigrette and serve immediately.

Dark Chocolate Ganache with Orange Zest.name: Dark Chocolate Ganache with Orange Zest
Dark Chocolate Ganache with Orange Zest.description: A classic pairing where the bright essential oils of the orange zest lift the deep, earthy bitterness of dark chocolate.
instructions: Place chopped chocolate in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat cream and orange zest until just simmering, then remove from heat and let steep for 10 minutes.
Re-warm the cream slightly if needed, then pour over the chocolate.
Let sit for 3 minutes, then gently whisk from the center outwards until smooth and glossy.
Pour into serving vessels and top with flaky sea salt. Chill until set.

Ginger-Orange Glazed Duck.name: Ginger-Orange Glazed Duck
Ginger-Orange Glazed Duck.description: A savory-sweet centerpiece where the pungent ginger and zesty orange cut through the rich fat of the duck.
instructions: Roast the duck at 350°F (175°C) for 1.5 hours, pricking the skin to release fat.
In a saucepan, combine orange juice, ginger, soy sauce, and honey. Simmer until reduced by half.
Brush the duck with the glaze every 10 minutes during the last 30 minutes of roasting.
Rest the duck for 15 minutes before carving.
allergen_warnings: Wheat/Gluten (Soy Sauce - unless Tamari is used)

Vanilla Bean Panna Cotta with Strawberry.name: Vanilla Bean Panna Cotta with Strawberry Compote
Vanilla Bean Panna Cotta with Strawberry.description: A creamy, delicate dessert that acts as a perfect canvas for the sweet-tart strawberry topping.
ingredients_list: 1 cup Strawberries, cooked with 1 tbsp sugar
instructions: Bloom gelatin in 2 tablespoons of cold water.
In a saucepan, heat cream, sugar, and vanilla bean (seeds and pod) until gently simmering.
Remove from heat, discard the vanilla pod, and whisk in the bloomed gelatin until dissolved.
Divide mixture into ramekins and chill for 4 hours until set.
Serve topped with the chilled strawberry compote.

Butter-Roasted Apples with Vanilla Miso.name: Butter-Roasted Apples with Vanilla Miso
Butter-Roasted Apples with Vanilla Miso.description: An umami-rich dessert where savory miso paste unexpectedly enhances the sweet, floral notes of vanilla and baked apple.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Melt the butter, then whisk in the miso paste, vanilla extract, and brown sugar until smooth.
Brush the mixture generously over the cut sides of the apples.
Roast for 30-40 minutes until tender, brushing with pan juices halfway through.

Vanilla-Infused Brown Butter Scallops.name: Vanilla-Infused Brown Butter Scallops
Vanilla-Infused Brown Butter Scallops.description: A luxurious savory dish where the subtle floral woody notes of vanilla bridge the sweet ocean flavor of the scallops.
instructions: Season scallops with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Sear scallops for 2 minutes per side until deeply crusted.
In the last minute of cooking, add the vanilla bean seeds to the butter. It will foam and brown.
Remove scallops. Add lemon juice to the brown butter to deglaze.
Drizzle the vanilla brown butter over the scallops to serve.

Strawberry and Basil Seed Pudding.name: Strawberry and Basil Seed Pudding
Strawberry and Basil Seed Pudding.description: A textured, vibrant breakfast pudding combining the chia-like gel of basil seeds with fresh strawberry puree.
instructions: Mix the basil seeds with the almond milk and sweetener. Let sit for 15 minutes to gel, stirring once.
Layer the basil seed pudding in a glass with the fresh strawberry puree.
Refrigerate for an hour before eating to allow flavors to meld.

Classic Caprese with Aged Balsamic.name: Classic Caprese with Aged Balsamic
Classic Caprese with Aged Balsamic.description: The quintessential Italian salad showcasing the perfection of fresh basil alongside sweet tomatoes and creamy mozzarella.
instructions: Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a platter.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the slices.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and aged balsamic vinegar.
Finish with flaky sea salt just before serving.

Lemon-Basil Sorbet.name: Lemon-Basil Sorbet
Lemon-Basil Sorbet.description: An incredibly refreshing palate cleanser where herbaceous basil tempers the sharp acidity of lemon.
instructions: Make a simple syrup by boiling water and sugar until dissolved. Remove from heat.
Add basil leaves to the hot syrup and steep for 30 minutes. Strain.
Mix the basil-infused syrup with the freshly squeezed lemon juice.
Chill the mixture thoroughly, then churn in an ice cream maker according to manufacturer instructions.

Spiced Braised Red Cabbage.name: Spiced Braised Red Cabbage
Spiced Braised Red Cabbage.description: A deeply flavorful side dish where the robust warmth of clove permeates the sweet and sour braised cabbage.
instructions: Melt butter in a large pot. Add shredded cabbage and grated apple, sautéing until slightly softened.
Add vinegar, brown sugar, and whole cloves. Stir well.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 45-60 minutes until very tender.
Remove the whole cloves before serving.

Orange and Clove Glazed Ham.name: Orange and Clove Glazed Ham
Orange and Clove Glazed Ham.description: A holiday classic. Whole cloves studded into the ham infuse the meat with deep spice, while the orange glaze provides necessary acidity.
instructions: Score the fat of the ham in a diamond pattern. Press a whole clove into the center of each diamond.
Bake the ham at 325°F (160°C) until warmed through (about 15 mins per pound).
Whisk marmalade, mustard, and brown sugar to create the glaze.
Brush the glaze over the ham heavily during the last 30 minutes of baking, until caramelized.

Clove-Spiced Poached Pears.name: Clove-Spiced Poached Pears
Clove-Spiced Poached Pears.description: An elegant dessert where pears gently absorb the complex, warm medicinal spice of clove and wine.
ingredients_list: 4 Bosc Pears, peeled with stems on
instructions: In a deep saucepan, combine wine, sugar, cloves, and cinnamon stick. Bring to a simmer.
Submerge the peeled pears in the liquid. Cover with a parchment paper cartouche to keep them submerged.
Simmer gently for 20-30 minutes until tender.
Remove pears. Boil the remaining liquid until reduced to a thick syrup. Serve pears drizzled with the syrup.

Dark Chocolate and Orange Truffles.name: Dark Chocolate and Orange Truffles
Dark Chocolate and Orange Truffles.description: Bite-sized confections that perfectly balance the bitterness of cocoa with the brightness of orange essence.
instructions: Place chocolate in a bowl. Scald the heavy cream and pour over the chocolate. Let sit for 3 minutes, then whisk until smooth.
Stir in the orange liqueur.
Refrigerate the mixture for 2 hours until firm.
Scoop into small balls, roll in your hands, then toss in cocoa powder to coat. Keep refrigerated.

Chili-Infused Dark Chocolate Tart.name: Chili-Infused Dark Chocolate Tart
Chili-Infused Dark Chocolate Tart.description: A sophisticated dessert where the capsaicin heat slowly builds, complementing the deep earthy notes of the chocolate.
instructions: Simmer the cream with the cayenne pepper and cinnamon. Remove from heat and let steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the infused cream, reheat slightly, and pour over the chopped dark chocolate.
Whisk until a smooth ganache forms. Pour into the prepared tart shell.
Chill for 3 hours until set before slicing.

Mocha Panna Cotta.name: Mocha Panna Cotta
Mocha Panna Cotta.description: A flawless pairing of coffee and dark chocolate flavors in a silky Italian dessert format.
instructions: Bloom gelatin in cold water.
Heat cream and sugar until simmering. Remove from heat and whisk in the espresso powder and chopped chocolate until smooth.
Whisk in the bloomed gelatin.
Pour into molds and refrigerate for 4 hours until firm.

Coffee-Rubbed Steak.name: Coffee-Rubbed Steak
Coffee-Rubbed Steak.description: The deep roasted, bitter notes of coffee perfectly mirror the Maillard reactions of seared beef.
instructions: Mix ground coffee, brown sugar, paprika, salt, and pepper to create a rub.
Generously coat the steak in the rub and let sit at room temp for 30 mins.
Pan-sear in cast iron over high heat until crusted on both sides.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

Beef Skewers with Garlic-Peppercorn Glaz.name: Beef Skewers with Garlic-Peppercorn Glaze
Beef Skewers with Garlic-Peppercorn Glaz.description: Savory beef heightened by pungent roasted garlic and the sharp piney bite of black pepper.
instructions: Thread beef onto skewers.
Whisk garlic, black pepper, soy sauce, and honey to create the glaze.
Grill skewers over high heat, brushing constantly with the glaze until charred.
Serve immediately.

Umami Burger with Red Wine Jam.name: Umami Burger with Red Wine Jam
Umami Burger with Red Wine Jam.description: An intense, savory profile balancing rich beef fat with the dark fruited notes of reduced red wine.
instructions: Simmer onions in olive oil until deeply caramelized.
Deglaze with red wine and balsamic vinegar, reducing until jammy.
Grill the beef patty to desired doneness.
Assemble burger topped with a thick layer of the red wine jam.

Salmon Crudo with Dill & Lemon.name: Salmon Crudo with Dill & Lemon
Salmon Crudo with Dill & Lemon.description: Raw salmon whose fresh marine notes are elevated by vibrant lemon and herbaceous dill.
instructions: Thinly slice the salmon and arrange on a chilled plate.
Whisk olive oil and lemon juice, then drizzle over the fish.
Top with fresh dill, lemon zest, and flaky sea salt immediately before serving.

Soy-Glazed Salmon Bowl.name: Soy-Glazed Salmon Bowl
Soy-Glazed Salmon Bowl.description: Rich, fatty salmon caramelized with deep umami soy sauce.
instructions: Whisk soy sauce, mirin, and sugar to make the glaze.
Broil the salmon on high, brushing generously with the glaze every 2 minutes.
Serve the caramelized salmon over warm white rice.

Smoked Salmon and Carrot Ribbons.name: Smoked Salmon and Carrot Ribbons
Smoked Salmon and Carrot Ribbons.description: Earthy, sweet carrots offer a brilliant textural and flavor contrast to salty, smoky salmon.
instructions: Use a vegetable peeler to shave carrots into long, thin ribbons.
Toss ribbons with lemon juice and olive oil to lightly pickle.
Wrap ribbons loosely around folds of smoked salmon.
Garnish with chopped chives.

Roasted Garlic Butter Steak.name: Roasted Garlic Butter Steak
Roasted Garlic Butter Steak.description: A classic pairing; pungent, sulfuric garlic rounds out the deep metallic savoriness of steak.
instructions: Squeeze roasted garlic cloves from their skins and mash into the softened butter along with chopped thyme.
Sear steak over high heat until a crust forms.
Top the hot steak with a generous dollop of the roasted garlic butter as it rests.

Tomato and Garlic Confit.name: Tomato and Garlic Confit
Tomato and Garlic Confit.description: Slow-cooking mellows garlic's bite into a sweet paste while deeply enriching the umami of the cherry tomatoes.
ingredients_list: 1 head Garlic, cloves separated and peeled
instructions: Place tomatoes and peeled garlic cloves into a small baking dish.
Submerge completely in olive oil.
Bake at 250°F (120°C) for 2 hours until garlic is meltingly soft.
Store in the oil and use over crusty bread.

Mushroom & Garlic Aglio e Olio.name: Mushroom & Garlic Aglio e Olio
Mushroom & Garlic Aglio e Olio.description: Earthy mushrooms act as sponges, soaking in the sharp, pungent sulfur compounds of gently sautéed garlic.
instructions: Boil pasta until al dente.
Gently warm olive oil over medium-low heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cooking until garlic is lightly golden.
Add mushrooms and sauté until browned.
Toss cooked pasta in the garlic-mushroom oil, adding pasta water to emulsify.

Roasted Carrots with Orange & Ginger Gla.name: Roasted Carrots with Orange & Ginger Glaze
Roasted Carrots with Orange & Ginger Gla.description: The bright citrus and sharp ginger elevate the deep, sweet earthiness of the roasted root vegetable.
instructions: Toss carrots in olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast at 400°F (200°C) for 25 mins.
Simmer orange juice, ginger, and honey until syrupy.
Toss the hot roasted carrots in the glaze before serving.

Spiced Carrot Soup with Cinnamon.name: Spiced Carrot Soup with Cinnamon
Spiced Carrot Soup with Cinnamon.description: The warm, sweet spice of cinnamon enhances the natural sugars of the carrot in this velvety soup.
instructions: Sauté onions and carrots until softened.
Add cinnamon and toast for 1 minute.
Pour in broth and simmer until carrots are completely tender (about 20 mins).
Blend until smooth, then stir in heavy cream.

Carrot Ribbon Salad with Salmon.name: Carrot Ribbon Salad with Salmon
Carrot Ribbon Salad with Salmon.description: Earthy, sweet carrots offer a brilliant textural and flavor contrast to salty, smoky salmon.
instructions: Use a vegetable peeler to shave carrots into long, thin ribbons.
Toss ribbons with lemon juice and olive oil to lightly pickle.
Wrap ribbons loosely around folds of smoked salmon.
Garnish with chopped chives.

Cinnamon Spiced Carrot Cake.name: Cinnamon Spiced Carrot Cake
Cinnamon Spiced Carrot Cake.description: The quintessential pairing of earthy sweet carrot and warm baking spices.
instructions: Whisk flour, cinnamon, sugar, and rising agents.
Fold in wet ingredients and grated carrots.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 35-40 minutes.
Cool completely before layering with cream cheese frosting.

Mexican Hot Chocolate.name: Mexican Hot Chocolate
Mexican Hot Chocolate.description: The warmth of cinnamon and chili perfectly accentuates the robust bitterness of dark cacao.
instructions: Heat milk in a saucepan until simmering.
Whisk in chopped chocolate, cinnamon, and cayenne until totally melted and frothy.
Serve immediately.

Vanilla & Cinnamon Poached Apples.name: Vanilla & Cinnamon Poached Apples
Vanilla & Cinnamon Poached Apples.description: A comforting dessert where sweet vanilla bridges the gap between aromatic cinnamon and tart apples.
instructions: Combine water, sugar, vanilla, and the cinnamon stick in a pot. Bring to a simmer.
Add apples, cover with a cartouche, and poach gently for 20 minutes.
Serve apples warm, drizzled with the reduced poaching syrup.

Cacio e Pepe.name: Cacio e Pepe
Cacio e Pepe.description: A masterclass in minimalism where the sharp, pungent bite of black pepper cuts perfectly through rich, salty sheep's milk cheese.
ingredients_list: 1 tbsp Whole Black Peppercorns, freshly toasted and crushed
instructions: Boil pasta until al dente, reserving 1 cup of starchy pasta water.
Toast crushed pepper in a dry skillet until fragrant.
Whisk the Pecorino with a splash of hot pasta water in a bowl to form a thick paste.
Toss the pasta and pepper in the skillet, slowly adding the cheese paste and more water until a creamy emulsion forms.

Pepper-Crusted Beef Steak.name: Pepper-Crusted Beef Steak
Pepper-Crusted Beef Steak.description: Steak au poivre style; a thick crust of black pepper provides intense heat that contrasts the rich fat and Maillard compounds of the beef.
instructions: Heavily salt the steak, then press the crushed black peppercorns firmly onto both sides to form a crust.
Heat oil in a cast iron skillet until smoking.
Sear steak for 3-4 minutes per side. Add butter in the final minute and baste to toast the pepper without burning.
Rest before slicing.
